stem: A urban planning study measures the commute time (minutes) for a sample of 30 city workers. The sample mean is 166.9 min with a sample standard deviation of 27.5 min. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 166.9 min. 95% CI = [156.6, 177.2]. Point estimate: 166.9 min.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
